Rights.Note: CC0 1.0 Universal is the Creative Commons license applicable to all publicly available SPASE metadata descriptions

ResourceHeader.ResourceName: STEREO-A WAVES Low Frequency Receiver L3A DEF data
ResourceHeader.Description:
This data set contains definitive goniopolarimetry parameters (level 3A) derived from STEREO-A WAVES LFR (Low Frequency Receiver, 2.5 - 160 kHz).
Goniopolarimetry data are computed asssuming: (i) incoming wave properties do not change over three successive antennas-pair measurements, (ii) signals measured by an antenna are the same on the two receiver channels, (iii) radio sources have an uniform brightness distribution.
ResourceHeader.Acknowledgement: Data provided are publicly available. If used in presentations or publications, please acknowledge STEREO/Waves Investigation (M. Maksimovic and S. D. Bale, PIs). We recommend the data users to contact the STEREO/Waves team early in their study. For reporting purposes, we request bibliography information for any publication, etc., using these data. Please send information on the use of this data to: M. Maksimovic (PI). We suggest to add the following lines into the acknowledgments: The authors acknowledge CNES (Centre National d'Etudes Spatiales), CNRS (Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique), and Observatoire de Paris for support to the STEREO/Waves team and the CDPP (Centre de Données de la Physique des Plasmas) for the provision of the STEREO/Waves data. The following papers should also be used to reference: - Bougeret et al, Space Science Reviews, 2008, doi:10.1007/s11214-007-9298-8 - Krupar et al., Journal of Geophysical Research, 2012, doi:10.1029/2011JA017333

PublicationInfo.Authors: Krupar, V., Q. N. Nguyen, X. Bonnin, B. Cecconi & M. Maksimovic

AccessInformation.Acknowledgement:
Data provided are available under CC-BY licence. Any use of this dataset in presentations or publications require citation, including the dataset DOI, such as, e.g.: Krupar, V., Q.N. Nguyen, X. Bonnin, B. Cecconi & M. Maksimovic (2022). STEREO/Waves/LFR-HFR L3 DF Dataset (Version 1.0) [Dataset]. PADC. https://doi.org/10.25935/4TAK-5225
The STEREO/Waves Investigation (M. Maksimovic and S. D. Bale, PIs) shall be acknowledged as follows: "The authors acknowledge CNES (Centre National d'Etudes Spatiales), CNRS (Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique), and Observatoire de Paris for support to the STEREO/Waves team, as well as the MASER (Measuring, Analyzing & Simulating Emissions in the Radio range) repository and PADC (Paris Astronomical Data Centre) for the provision of the STEREO/Waves data." We recommend the data users to contact the STEREO/Waves team early in their study. For reporting purposes, we request bibliography information for any publication, etc., using these data. Please send information on the use of this data to: M. Maksimovic (PI). The following papers should also be used to reference: - Bougeret et al, Space Science Reviews, 2008, doi:10.1007/s11214-007-9298-8 - Krupar et al., Journal of Geophysical Research, 2012, doi:10.1029/2011JA017333

Parameter.Name: Epoch (Time)
Parameter.Description: Epoch encoded as Terrestrial Time on rotating Earth geoid, ns since J2000

Parameter.Name: FREQUENCY
Parameter.Description: Frequency channel

Parameter.Name: BACKGROUND
Parameter.Description: 1% receiver background of each antenna channel configuration

Parameter.Name: SENSOR_CONFIG
Parameter.Description: Dual channel receiver antenna configuration. 00 (Off/Off) 10 (+Ex/Off) 20 (-Ey/Off) 30 (Ex-Ey/Off) 01 (Off/+Ez) 02 (Off/+Ey) 03 (Off/Ez-Ey) 11 (+Ex/+Ez) 12 (+Ex/-Ey) 13 (+Ex/Ez-Ey) 21 (-Ey/+Ez) 31 (Ex-Ey/+Ez) -22 (-Ey/-Ey) -32 (Ex-Ey/-Ey) -23 (-Ey/Ez-Ey) -33 (Ex-Ey/Ez-Ey)

Parameter.Name: TIMING
Parameter.Description: Temporal offset of each antenna channel configuration

Parameter.Name: QUALITY_FLAG
Parameter.Description: High level information about the quality of the electric field measurements. All flags are encoded as follows: QUALITY_FLAG = 0, bad data QUALITY_FLAG = 1, known problems use at your own risk QUALITY_FLAG = 2, survey data, possibly not publication quality QUALITY_FLAG = 3, good for publication subject to PI approval QUALITY_FLAG = 4, Excellent data which has received special treatment

Parameter.Name: INTERPOL_FLAG
Parameter.Description: Flag that indicates if the current record is real or interpolated. All flags are encoded as follows: INTERPOL_FLAG = 0, record is real INTERPOL_FLAG = 1, record is interpolated

Parameter.Name: PSD_V2
Parameter.Description: Power spectral density before 1% background subtraction and antenna calibration. Suitable for analysis of weak signals.

Parameter.Name: PSD_FLUX
Parameter.Description: Power spectral density after 1% background subtraction and antenna calibration. Suitable for general investigation.

Parameter.Name: PSD_SFU
Parameter.Description: Power spectral density after 1% background subtraction and antenna calibration in solar radio flux unit normalized to 1 au. Suitable for multi-spacecraft investigation.

Parameter.Name: STOKES_I
Parameter.Description: Radio flux density. Details on polarization parameters can be found here: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11214-007-9255-6.

Parameter.Name: STOKES_Q
Parameter.Description: Normalized linear polarization. Details on polarization parameters can be found here: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11214-007-9255-6.

Parameter.Name: STOKES_U
Parameter.Description: Normalized linear polarization. Details on polarization parameters can be found here: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11214-007-9255-6.

Parameter.Name: STOKES_V
Parameter.Description: Normalized circular polarization. Details on polarization parameters can be found here: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11214-007-9255-6.

Parameter.Name: SOURCE_SIZE
Parameter.Description: The angular half aperture of the source as seen by a spacecraft (uniform profile). Based on https://doi.org/10.1029/2011JA017333.

Parameter.Name: WAVE_COLATITUDE_RTN
Parameter.Description: Wave vector colatitude in the Radial-Tangential-Normal (RTN) system. X axis points from the spacecraft center to Sun, and the Y axis is the cross product of the solar rotational axis and X, and lies in the solar equatorial plane (towards the West limb).

Parameter.Name: WAVE_AZIMUTH_RTN
Parameter.Description: Wave vector azimuth in the Radial-Tangential-Normal (RTN) system. X axis points from the spacecraft center to Sun, and the Y axis is the cross product of the solar rotational axis and X, and lies in the solar equatorial plane (towards the West limb).

Parameter.Name: WAVE_COLATITUDE_HCI
Parameter.Description: Wave vector colatitude in the Heliocentric Inertial (HCI) system. Z is the solar north rotational axis, and X is the solar ascending node on the J2000 ecliptic.

Parameter.Name: WAVE_AZIMUTH_HCI
Parameter.Description: Wave vector azimuth in the Heliocentric Inertial (HCI) system. Z is the solar north rotational axis, and X is the solar ascending node on the J2000 ecliptic.

Parameter.Name: WAVE_COLATITUDE_HEE
Parameter.Description: Wave vector colatitude in the Heliocentric Earth Ecliptic (HEE) system. X is the Sun-Earth line, and Z is the north pole for the ecliptic of date.

Parameter.Name: WAVE_AZIMUTH_HEE
Parameter.Description: Wave vector azimuth in the Heliocentric Earth Ecliptic (HEE) system. X is the Sun-Earth line, and Z is the north pole for the ecliptic of date.

Parameter.Name: WAVE_COLATITUDE_HEEQ
Parameter.Description: Wave vector colatitude in the Heliocentric Earth Equatorial (HEEQ) system. Z is the solar rotation axis, and X is in the plane containing the Z axis and Earth, at the intersection of the solar central meridian, and the heliographic equator. When converted to longitude and latitude, this is known as Stonyhurst heliographic coordinates.

Parameter.Name: WAVE_AZIMUTH_HEEQ
Parameter.Description: Wave vector azimuth in the Heliocentric Earth Equatorial (HEEQ) system. Z is the solar rotation axis, and X is in the plane containing the Z axis and Earth, at the intersection of the solar central meridian, and the heliographic equator. When converted to longitude and latitude, this is known as Stonyhurst heliographic coordinates.

Parameter.Name: SC_POS_HCI
Parameter.Description: STEREO spacecraft position in the Heliocentric Inertial (HCI) system in km. Also called Ecliptic J2000. Z is the solar north rotational axis, and X is the solar ascending node on the J2000 ecliptic.

Parameter.Name: SC_POS_HEE
Parameter.Description: STEREO spacecraft position in the Heliocentric Earth Ecliptic (HEE) system in km. X is the Sun-Earth line, and Z is the north pole for the ecliptic of date.

Parameter.Name: SC_POS_HEEQ
Parameter.Description: STEREO spacecraft position in the Heliocentric Earth Equatorial (HEEQ) system in km.
